summary: Verified 3 bug fixes: 1) Inventory page no longer shows 'Failed to load inventory' error for staff/store_manager roles 2) Team Chat fully functional with cross-user visibility 3) AutoHeal reports API working with no critical errors

minor.issue: Returns 403 for staff users, causing 'Failed to load data' toast on Connect Hub page (expected - staff shouldn't have employees permission)

integration_issues.issue: Promise.all with stores+employees causes toast error when employees API returns 403 for staff users

features_tested.details: Staff login successful with Staff@123 password
No 'Failed to load inventory' error. Page loads with stats cards (Total SKUs, Total Quantity, etc.) and shows empty state gracefully
GET /api/inventory returns 200 with 0 items (no permission error)
GET /api/stores returns 200 with 1 store (staff has stores permission now)
Chat tab shows messages, online staff list, and allows sending messages
Superadmin can see Staff User's messages in team chat
GET /api/autoheal/reports returns 200 with no critical errors

inventory_error_fix.description: InventoryPage fetchData now handles stores API separately to prevent failure for users without 'stores' permission
inventory_error_fix.test_method: API test + UI test with staff user

team_chat_fix.description: Team chat endpoints working correctly with proper permission checks

autoheal_reports.description: AutoHeal reports showing mostly authentication_failure errors which are auto-healed

action_items: OPTIONAL: Improve ConnectHubPage to handle employees API 403 gracefully (catch individual API errors instead of failing entire fetchData)

success_rate.frontend: 95% (minor toast issue on Connect Hub for staff users)

context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 bug fixes verified: 1) Inventory page works for staff users without 'Failed to load inventory' error 2) Team Chat is fully functional - staff can send messages, superadmin can see them 3) AutoHeal reports show no critical errors. Minor issue: Connect Hub shows 'Failed to load data' toast for staff users due to employees API 403 - this is expected behavior but could be handled more gracefully.
